location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in-office; Wednesday optional WFH; Friday depends on work type

secondary_archetype: Enterprise / Revenue Technology Product Manager (internal IT/systems PM)

level_signals: 10+ years of experience in Product Management, Revenue Technology, Business Systems, or related GTM technology domains
Lead a team of Product Owners
Experience mentoring product managers or product owners
Proven ability to lead large-scale cross-functional initiatives and influence executive stakeholders
Comp range CAD 178,000–209,000 consistent with senior individual contributor / lead level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in Product Management, Revenue Technology, Business Systems, or related GTM technology domains in high-growth SaaS or enterprise environments.
Deep expertise with Salesforce CRM and adjacent revenue platforms supporting sales and quote-to-cash operations.

Strong understanding of enterprise sales processes and revenue operations, including Revenue Planning, Account & Contact Management, Opportunity Management, Forecasting, Territory Management, Sales Compensation, CPQ and Quote-to-Cash processes, Contract Lifecycle Management, Billing and Provisioning workflows.
Proven ability to lead large-scale cross-functional initiatives and influence executive stakeholders, business leaders, and technical teams.
Experience mentoring product managers or product owners in fast-paced and evolving environments.

Strong strategic thinking, problem-solving, and analytical capabilities with the ability to translate business needs into scalable system and process solutions. Comfortable working across technical architecture, integrations, data models, and operational processes.

Experience partnering with Engineering, Data, RevOps, and Business Systems teams to design and implement enterprise-grade solutions. Ability to leverage analytics, user research, and operational data to drive prioritization and product decisions.
Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and organizational skills with a proven ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver results in complex environments.
Degree in Computer Science, Business Management, or a related field.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making

scope.team_size_estimate: leads a team of Product Owners (size not specified)
scope.surface: Revenue Technology (RevTech) ecosystem — CRM, CPQ, forecasting, territory management, compensation, contract lifecycle management, billing, and provisioning systems
scope.business_model: internal enterprise technology / IT product management within a SaaS company (B2B SaaS)
core_responsibilities: Define and drive the product vision and strategy for Asana's Revenue Technology ecosystem
Develop and maintain a multi-year product roadmap across CRM, forecasting, territory management, compensation, CPQ, contracting, billing, and provisioning systems
Lead modernization and optimization of core revenue workflows and systems
Drive adoption of AI-powered and automation-first capabilities across the revenue technology stack
Establish KPIs, operational metrics, and reporting frameworks to measure product adoption and revenue impact
Serve as the connective layer across Sales, RevOps, Finance, Channel, Legal, CX, Data, and Engineering teams
Evaluate and manage strategic technology platforms and vendor partnerships
Lead a team of Product Owners and drive Core pillar capability development

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as additional component of compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated in CAD (178,000–209,000)
Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Clear product surface and ownership area (Revenue Technology ecosystem)
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every listed requirement
Asana recognized as Fortune Best Workplaces 7+ years — culture signal
Specific office days stated upfront (Mon/Tue/Thu), reducing ambiguity about hybrid expectations
red_flags: Role is Vancouver-office-centric hybrid (Mon/Tue/Thu in office) — eliminates remote candidates outside Vancouver
Compensation stated in CAD only; USD equivalent not provided, limiting easy comparison for US-based candidates
High buzzword density makes it harder to identify the true scope and differentiation of the role

The 'Enterprise Product Manager' title is atypical — this role functions more like a Director-level internal systems/IT PM managing Product Owners, which may not match standard PM career path expectations
10+ years hard requirement with deep Salesforce and QTC expertise narrows the candidate pool significantly
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not addressed anywhere in the JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 10+ years requirement, team management, and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'lead a team of Product Owners')
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and internal RevTech context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not a direct quote)
compensation.base_range_usd (null — only CAD range stated; not converted to USD)
ambiguities: The role title 'Enterprise Product Manager' typically implies an external-facing product role, but the JD describes an internal IT/Enterprise Technology systems PM managing Revenue Technology platforms — more akin to a Director of Revenue Technology or Enterprise Systems PM.
Friday work-from-home is conditional ('depends on the type of work you do and the teams with which you partner') — actual in-office requirement is ambiguous for Fridays.
Education requirement says 'Degree' without specifying bachelor's vs. master's; treated as bachelor's-level minimum but could be interpreted more broadly.

The JD does not state whether the role manages direct reports beyond Product Owners or if it has people management (performance review) responsibilities — inferred as yes based on 'lead a team of Product Owners' and mentoring language.
